questions.question: The resting membrane potential of a neuron is approximately:
The neurotransmitter at cholinergic synapses is:
The part of the brain responsible for maintaining balance and coordination is:
Which type of neuron carries impulses FROM receptors TO CNS?
The blind spot in the eye corresponds to:
The knee-jerk reflex arc involves:
The diagram shows an action potential. What happens at threshold potential?
questions.diagram_description: Graph of action potential showing membrane potential (mV) vs time (ms): resting potential at -70mV, threshold at -55mV (where Na+ channels open), depolarization phase rising to +40mV, repolarization (K+ channels open), after-hyperpolarization below -70mV (refractory period), return to resting. Each phase labeled with ionic events

options.B: Voltage-gated Na+ channels open causing rapid depolarization
options.D: K+ ions rush into the cell

questions.question: The eustachian tube connects:
Saltatory conduction occurs in:

options.B: Myelinated nerve fibres (impulse jumps between Nodes of Ranvier)

questions.question: The hypothalamus controls:

options.B: Body temperature, hunger, sleep, and endocrine functions

questions.question: The diagram shows the structure of the human eye. Which structure focuses light onto the retina?
questions.diagram_description: Sagittal section of human eye showing: cornea (transparent outer layer, does most refraction), aqueous humor, iris with pupil, lens (biconvex, adjustable focus/accommodation), vitreous humor, retina (photoreceptor layer), macula with fovea (highest acuity), optic disc/blind spot, optic nerve, choroid (vascular layer), sclera (white outer coat)

questions.question: GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid) is a:
The optic chiasma is where:

options.B: Nasal (medial) fibres of both optic nerves cross to opposite side

questions.question: The outer ear consists of:

options.B: Pinna, external auditory canal, tympanic membrane

questions.question: Conduction velocity is highest in which nerve fibre type?
The diagram shows the reflex arc. Which component receives and processes the signal to produce a response?
questions.diagram_description: Diagram of reflex arc showing: receptor (skin/muscle spindle) → afferent (sensory) neuron → nerve centre in spinal cord (interneuron/integration center, shown as synapse in grey matter) → efferent (motor) neuron → effector (muscle/gland). Spinal cord cross-section shown with grey matter (butterfly shape) and white matter around it

questions.question: Rod cells in the retina are responsible for:
Meninges are the protective membranes covering the CNS. Their correct order from outside to inside is:
The sodium-potassium pump maintains resting potential by pumping:

options.B: 3 Na+ out and 2 K+ in per ATP
options.C: Equal Na+ and K+ in opposite directions

questions.question: Wernicke's area is associated with:
The diagram shows the cochlea. Which part contains the organ of Corti?
questions.diagram_description: Cross-section of cochlea (unrolled) showing three fluid-filled canals: Scala vestibuli (above basilar membrane, contains perilymph), Scala media/cochlear duct (between, contains endolymph, bounded by Reissner's membrane above and basilar membrane below), Scala tympani (below basilar membrane, contains perilymph). Organ of Corti labeled on basilar membrane in scala media, with hair cells, tectorial membrane above

options.C: Scala media (cochlear duct) on basilar membrane

questions.question: Multiple sclerosis involves:

options.A: Destruction of motor neuron cell bodies

questions.question: The pupil of the eye constricts (miosis) due to:
Cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) is produced by:
Absolute refractory period in neurons means:

options.B: No action potential can be generated regardless of stimulus strength

questions.question: The diagram shows synaptic transmission. What happens when EPSP (excitatory post-synaptic potential) summation reaches threshold?
questions.diagram_description: Diagram of synapse showing presynaptic terminal with vesicles, synaptic cleft, post-synaptic membrane with receptors. Graph showing: subthreshold EPSP1 (small depolarization), subthreshold EPSP2, temporal summation (EPSP1+EPSP2 reaching threshold → action potential). Spatial summation also shown. Threshold line marked. IPSP (hyperpolarization) also shown for inhibitory synapse

options.A: An action potential is generated in the post-synaptic neuron

questions.question: Night blindness is caused by deficiency of:

options.B: Vitamin A (retinol, needed to make rhodopsin)

questions.question: The autonomic nervous system is divided into:
Hair cells in the semicircular canals detect:
The corpus callosum connects:
The diagram shows the retina layers. Which cells convert light to electrical signals (phototransduction)?
questions.diagram_description: Cross-section of retina showing layers from outside (choroid) inward: (1) Pigment epithelium; (2) Photoreceptors – rods (dim light, peripheral) and cones (color, fovea); (3) Bipolar cells; (4) Ganglion cells; (5) Optic nerve fibres → optic disc. Light enters from ganglion cell side and reaches photoreceptors at back

questions.question: Alzheimer's disease is associated with:

options.B: Amyloid plaques and tau tangles causing neuron death

questions.question: The thalamus acts as:

options.B: Relay station for sensory signals to cerebral cortex

questions.question: Accommodation of the eye for near vision involves:

options.B: Rounding of the lens (ciliary muscles contract)

questions.question: Parkinson's disease is caused by:

options.B: Degeneration of dopaminergic neurons in substantia nigra

questions.question: The diagram shows the distribution of hearing along the basilar membrane. Where are high-frequency sounds detected?
questions.diagram_description: Diagram of unrolled cochlea showing basilar membrane with tonotopic organization: base of cochlea (near oval window) – narrow, stiff basilar membrane detects HIGH frequencies (20,000 Hz); apex of cochlea (helicotrema) – wide, flexible basilar membrane detects LOW frequencies (20 Hz). Frequency map shown along membrane with values

options.C: Base of cochlea near oval window

questions.question: The refractory period ensures:
Cones are most concentrated at:
Broca's area is involved in:
Acetylcholinesterase breaks down acetylcholine at:
The diagram shows the autonomic nervous system innervation of the heart. Which division slows heart rate?
questions.diagram_description: Diagram showing autonomic innervation: Sympathetic nervous system (thoracolumbar outflow T1–L2) – preganglionic neurons → sympathetic ganglia → postganglionic noradrenergic fibres → heart (increases HR, force). Parasympathetic nervous system (craniosacral outflow, vagus nerve CN X) – preganglionic fibres to intramural ganglia → short postganglionic cholinergic fibres → SA node (decreases HR, force)

questions.question: Which part of the ear contains the organ of Corti?
Glaucoma is caused by:

options.B: Increased intraocular pressure damaging optic nerve

questions.question: Action potential follows 'all or none' principle because:

options.A: Only one Na+ channel opens at a time
options.B: Once threshold is reached, voltage-gated Na+ channels fully open in a self-regenerating manner
options.C: All neurons have the same size

questions.question: The utricle and saccule of the inner ear detect:
The diagram shows the visual pathway. What is the visual field representation in the right occipital cortex?
questions.diagram_description: Diagram of visual pathway: Both eyes → optic nerves → optic chiasma (nasal fibres cross) → optic tract → lateral geniculate nucleus (thalamus) → optic radiation → primary visual cortex (striate cortex, V1) in occipital lobe. Right occipital cortex receives: temporal fibres of right eye (right visual field, right eye) + nasal fibres of left eye (also right visual field, left eye) = LEFT visual field of both eyes

options.A: Right visual field of both eyes
options.B: Left visual field of both eyes

questions.question: Which part of the brain is the 'seat of intelligence'?
The fovea of the eye has maximum visual acuity because:

options.B: High density of cones with 1:1 ratio to ganglion cells
options.C: It has both rods and cones equally
options.D: It is located at the optic disc

questions.question: Tetanus toxin causes continuous muscle spasms by:

options.B: Blocking inhibitory glycine/GABA neurotransmitter release at inhibitory synapses

questions.question: The primary auditory cortex is located in:
